weather.summary: Saturday: 11°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 12°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with fun activities for your little one! From affordable art fairs to puppet shows, there's something for everyone. Plus, with the weather being mild and overcast, it's a great time to explore both indoor and outdoor options in London!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Interactive museum designed for children with hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide range of activities suitable for a 3-year-old, including a Mini Museum for under 2s with sensory experiences. The play area encourages imagination and creativity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Check the daily schedule for workshops and storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play space with Story Worlds and a Story Garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers two floors of immersive play space and a Story Garden playground, perfect for active 3-year-olds. Baby Storytelling sessions available.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford. The centre is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend visits.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: Large park with playgrounds, ponds, and children's play areas.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers plenty of space to run around and play, plus playgrounds suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or walk to Victoria Park. Several bus routes serve the park.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy by the pond.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.address: No.1 Warehouse, West India Quay, London E14 4AL
activities.description: Learn about the history of London's waterfront through interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Features Mudlarks, an interactive gallery specifically designed for children. There is also a Soft Play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to West India Quay. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check for family-friendly events and activities.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Community farm with a variety of animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to see and interact with farm animals, which is always a hit with toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or take a short bus ride from central Hackney.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times to get the most out of your visit.

activities.title: Affordable Art Fair Family Mornings
activities.description: Dedicated morning sessions designed with children in mind with free drop-in creative activities, art packs and a Kids' Creative Corner.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers free creative activities and art packs, providing a hands-on experience suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a bus to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Arrive early to make the most of the free activities.

activities.title: Make-Lab Masterclass: Wind Machines at Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Use wire sculpting and foiling to design a wind turbine.
activities.whyItWorks: This activity focuses on wind turbines and is a hands-on activity for families.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Totally Tales at Chickenshed
activities.description: Show, Watch as the characters create a set story – and one made by you. Take your story ideas, toys or special items.
activities.whyItWorks: The show will involve stories that they create, and they are invited to bring toys to the show.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Southgate.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for any baby specific times

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the natural world with dinosaur skeletons, interactive exhibits, and fascinating displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide range of exhibits suitable for toddlers, including dinosaur skeletons and interactive displays.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits and displays on science and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: Features the Garden, an interactive space for young children to discover construction, water, light, and sound through play. The Pattern Pod is great for sensory exploration.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check for special events and demonstrations.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: Riverside hub of arts, fun, and food with free family activities and cultural events.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of free family events, including creative workshops and hands-on installations. Wide-open spaces for scooting and picnicking.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo or Embankment. The Southbank Centre is a short walk from either station.
transport.tip: Check the website for a detailed schedule of events.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers great views of London and greenery that provides a sensory experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Monument. The Sky Garden is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance, as entry is timed and limited.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: A small, free zoo with animals like wallabies and lemurs.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath, then a bus to Golders Hill Park.
transport.tip: Combine your visit with a walk on Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Hyde Park
activities.description: Large park with open spaces, the Serpentine lake, and playgrounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers plenty of space for running around, plus the Diana Memorial Playground in Kensington Gardens nearby.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Lancaster Gate, Marble Arch, or Hyde Park Corner.
transport.tip: Check for events and activities at the Hyde Park Learning Centre.

activities.title: British Library
activities.description: The UK's National Library with regular sessions for under 5s
activities.whyItWorks: British Library's long-awaited Fairy Tales exhibition opens from 27th March to 23rd August . Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, brought to life through books, theatre costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Euston Station
transport.tip: Check the schedule for any baby specific times

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: A great place for kids to let off some steam in central London. Features an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool (summer), and a small city farm.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Russell Square or King's Cross.
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry when accompanied by a child.

activities.title: Candlelight Concert for Babies
activities.description: Babies aged 0–9 months can relax to gentle classical music in a softly lit, candle-style setting. It's designed to be calm and sensory-friendly for tiny listeners.
activities.whyItWorks: Calm and sensory-friendly environment suitable for babies.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube.
transport.tip: Good activity for babies

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: Outdoor walking
activities.whyItWorks: Mostly outdoor walking – allow 2–3 hours if you're doing it properly. Head for Cherry Walk and Magnolia Walk, then finish near The Orangery café.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Richmond, then a bus to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Bring snacks

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Puppet show.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for small children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube.
transport.tip: Good activity for babies

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt – Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: An Easter trail that actually motivates kids to walk. You'll explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An outdoor energy-burner. Dress for wind. Bring snacks. Let them run.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court Palace
transport.tip: Bring snacks

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Enjoy a fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: There is a fun Easter adventure where kids explore and help the bees.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Richmond, then a bus to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland – Riverside Studios
activities.description: Tumble down the rabbit hole
activities.whyItWorks: Riverside Studios is showing Alice in Wonderland and is a great place to tumble down the rabbit hole.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hammersmith, then a bus to Riverside Studios
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to London Bridge, then a walk to Unicorn Theatre
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Show
activities.description: Take the kids along to Blackheath Halls for performances of The Very Hungry Caterpillar Show, an adaptation of four of Eric Carle's books. Naturally, that Caterpillar takes centre stage, joined by Brown Bear, Brown Bear, 10 Little Rubber Ducks and The Very Busy Spider. All are brought to life by more than 75 (!) puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: This show has an adaptation of four of Eric Carle's books, the kids will love it.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Blackheath Halls
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance
